contest.title: Neowise Labs Contest 1 (Codeforces Round 1018, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: choose each row/column increment at most once so that no side-adjacent grid cells have equal final heights.
Official tutorial confirms that row operations only affect vertical equalities, column operations only affect horizontal equalities, and each dimension is solved by 2-state DP.
Luogu solution cross-checks the independence of row and column decisions and the dpC/dpR two-state formulation.
Chinese writeup verifies the local transition rules based on differences -1, 0, and 1 between adjacent rows or columns.

annotation.summary: Choose a subset of rows and columns to increase by one at minimum cost so that every horizontally or vertically adjacent pair has different final heights.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 1000; an O(n^2) check of all adjacent row/column pairs with constant-size DP states is sufficient.
annotation.core_idea: Horizontal adjacent differences are unaffected by row operations, and vertical adjacent differences are unaffected by column operations. Therefore the row-worker decisions and column-worker decisions are two independent one-dimensional problems. For rows, let x_i in {0,1} indicate whether row i is incremented. A transition from x_{i-1}=y to x_i=x is allowed only if h_{i-1,j}+y != h_{i,j}+x for every column j; the transition cost adds a_i when x=1. Run this two-state DP over rows, run the same DP over columns with b_j and transposed adjacency, and add the two minima. If either dimension is impossible, return -1.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) time and O(n) or O(1) DP memory per test case
tricks: Separate horizontal constraints from vertical constraints.
Model each row or column decision as a binary variable.
Check each adjacent row/column transition by scanning the whole opposite dimension.

solution_variants.name: row-column-two-state-dp
solution_variants.summary: Solve rows and columns independently. For each adjacent pair, test the four choices of previous/current increment bit; if all cells in that adjacent pair remain unequal, relax the two-state DP with the current worker cost.

tags.evidence: Each one-dimensional subproblem has only two states per row or column: incremented or not incremented.
The key observation is that horizontal constraints depend only on column increments and vertical constraints depend only on row increments, so the grid splits into two independent DPs.
tags.description: Separate a multidimensional constraint system into independent lower-dimensional subproblems because each operation class affects only one family of constraints.
tags.created_reason: Existing DP tags describe the transition form but not the row/column independence observation that makes the problem simple.

tags.evidence: The implementation must carefully check the four binary transitions for each adjacent row or column pair.
